arts.ttl: “Typical” | Callum Wilson will return from injury to face West Ham
arts.sum: Newcastle United and England striker Callum Wilson will return from injury to face West Ham United tomorrow night. The 32-year-old hasn’t played for Newcastle so far in 24/25 after suffering back and hamstring injuries, however, according to Wilson himself, he will be returning to face his favourite opponents this weekend. There was concern amongst Geordies... The post “Typical” | Callum Wilson will return from injury to face West Ham appeared first on The West Ham Way.

arts.ttl: “Dismissed” | West Ham turned down chance to sign Dewsbury-Hall
arts.sum: West Ham United turned down the chance to sign Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall in the summer transfer window, according to Football Insider. Mick Brown, a scout at West Ham United under David Moyes has revealed he recommended that the Hammers make a move of Dewsbury-Hall after a fantastic season in the Championship with Leicester City. “I recommended... The post “Dismissed” | West Ham turned down chance to sign Dewsbury-Hall appeared first on The West Ham Way.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United transfer warning as striker decision confirmed amid Man Utd and Liverpool link
arts.sum: Newcastle have been reported as having an interest in Lille's Canadian international forward Jonathan David, a player enjoying an impressive season and one who has been tipped for stardom beyond Ligue1. Already the list of players being linked with a move to St James' Park is growing, with Eddie Howe keen to strengthen his squad in January for what is shaping up to be a big push for a Champions League place in what is an already congested Premier League table.

arts.ttl: “Shocking move” | Real Madrid interested in West Ham defender
arts.sum: Real Madrid could approach West Ham United in a move to sign Nayef Aguerd this winter, according to a report from Fichajes. Conference League winner Nayef Aguerd left the Hammers on loan this summer after two seasons and 58 appearances in Claret and Blue. The Morocco International secured a move to Spanish side Real Sociedad.... The post “Shocking move” | Real Madrid interested in West Ham defender appeared first on The West Ham Way.

arts.ttl: David Moyes to Everton update as former West Ham boss 'serious contender' amid takeover saga
arts.sum: Former West Ham United manager David Moyes is reportedly being eyed for a return to Premier League football and is on the radar to take over at rivals Everton. It is said the challenge would be an 'appealing' one for Moyes, so long as he receives the correct assurances over a contract, the new owners, their ambitions and the budget he would have to make transfers.

arts.ttl: I left Newcastle United in July and Leicester have now turned to me after Cooper sacking
arts.sum: Cooper's assistant, Alan Tate, and first-team coach and analyst Steve Rands have also left the club so training will now be overseen by Dawson, who will be supported by coaches Danny Alcock and Andy Hughes, as Leicester begin the process of appointing a new manager. Dawson, who left his role as first-team development coach at Newcastle four months ago, has been in a similar situation before.

arts.ttl: “Unlikely” | West Ham won’t make a move for Jose Mourinho
arts.sum: West Ham United are unlikely to make a move for Jose Mourinho if Julen Lopetegui is sacked, according to Football Insider sources. Speaking on the Inside Track podcast, FI correspondent Pete O’Rourke revealed that the Portuguese manager has switched on a charm offensive to circulate his name around clubs in the Premier League, despite being... The post “Unlikely” | West Ham won’t make a move for Jose Mourinho appeared first on The West Ham Way.

arts.ttl: Steve Cooper sacked by Leicester City with immediate effect ahead of Newcastle United clash
arts.sum: First Team Coach Ben Dawson is expected to take over training duties, assisted by Danny Alcock and Andy Hughes, while the club seeks to appoint a new manager promptly. This marked the Midlands team's sixth top-flight loss as they strive to secure their Premier League status after gaining promotion last season.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United to bank millions more after under the radar Premier League meeting development
arts.sum: Newcastle took home £72.2m from international broadcasters when the black-and-whites finished in fourth in 2023 - only Manchester City, Arsenal and Manchester United made more - and returning to the Champions League brought with it added exposure for Eddie Howe's team. Newcastle could be among those sides who actually stand to benefit most from this increase, if the Magpies continue to challenge the established order, as half the money from any rise in total overseas rights is distributed based on where a club finishes in the table.

arts.ttl: David Moyes could make Everton return to replace Sean Dyche once takeover completed
arts.sum: American businessman Dan Friedkin is looking to complete his takeover of Everton and former Toffees boss David Moyes could return as manager and replace Sean Dyche
